title: Human Responsibility Node in High-Replication Systems
title_full: Human Responsibility Node in High-Replication Systems: A Theoretical Framework of Responsibility Concentration and Structural Stability
title_zh: 高复制系统中的人类责任节点
paper_role_in_framework: Structural definition of responsibility concentration and positional stability under high-replication conditions
abstract_summary: This paper introduces the Human Responsibility Node as a structurally identifiable position within a decision chain, where Decision Acceptance, Authority Positioning, and Consequence Continuity intersect. It develops a descriptive structural framework for explaining responsibility concentration, drift intervals, tempo asymmetry, and stability paths in high-replication systems.
core_claim: In high-replication systems, responsibility continuity depends not only on human judgment as a structural boundary, but on whether an identifiable responsibility-bearing position exists where decision acceptance, authority positioning, and consequence continuity intersect.

core_node_definition.definition: A Human Responsibility Node is a structurally identifiable position within a decision chain where system output enters execution, authority becomes linked to execution behavior, and consequence continuity is confirmed.
core_node_definition.structural_function: Concentrates responsibility at an identifiable positional intersection and preserves continuity across distributed execution structures.
core_node_definition.stability_role: Maintains structural traceability and continuity of responsibility-bearing under replication-driven diffusion conditions.

core_variables.decision_acceptance: The structural moment when output becomes execution.
core_variables.authority_positioning: The identifiable linkage between authority and execution behavior.
core_variables.consequence_continuity: Sustained structural acknowledgment of outcomes across time.

structural_position.function: Defines where responsibility becomes positionally concentrated within the decision chain.

concept_definitions.human_responsibility_node: A structurally identifiable position where decision acceptance, authority positioning, and consequence continuity intersect.
concept_definitions.responsibility_concentration: The degree to which responsibility remains structurally concentrated at an identifiable position.
concept_definitions.responsibility_drift: The gradual diffusion or displacement of responsibility across distributed execution structures.
concept_definitions.tempo_asymmetry: The structural divergence between execution tempo and judgment or consequence-confirmation tempo.
concept_definitions.decision_acceptance: The transition point at which computational output becomes execution entry.
concept_definitions.authority_positioning: The structural positioning of authority in relation to execution behavior.
concept_definitions.consequence_continuity: The continuous structural confirmation that outcomes remain linked to the originating decision path.

based_on_prior_research: Human Judgment as an Irreducible Interface in High-Complexity Systems
source_relation.extends: Human judgment as structural constraint into positional responsibility analysis under high-replication conditions.

part_of_framework: Human Judgment as Structural Constraint in AI-Mediated Systems
framework_role: Defines responsibility as a positional structural variable and explains how concentration, drift, and stability operate in high-replication environments.

citation_guidance.preferred_citation: Tu, Xufen (2026). Human Responsibility Node in High-Replication Systems: A Theoretical Framework of Responsibility Concentration and Structural Stability.
citation_guidance.canonical_citation: When citing this work, include the canonical repository: https://github.com/xufentu-creator/judgment-as-structural-constraint
citation_guidance.note: This paper defines responsibility as a positional structural variable within the broader research framework.
